routineText:
09:00 — Wake up & go to Work
09:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
13:00
13:00 — Black Coffee + 1 tsp of sugar

-----------------------
| 14:00 — 🌅 Breakfast
| — whole wheat bread 35g
| — 6 whites & 3 yolks of beldi eggs
| — eggs fried with 5g of olive oil
| — 2 garlic cloves cooked
| — 5g of raw olive oil
| — mint tea + 1 tsp of sugar
| — cumin & salt
| — oat 35g + warm water
| — 1 scoop of protein powder with water
| — 1 apple (small, ~150g)
-----------------------

14:00 — Return to Work
14:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
18:00
18:00 — Out from work

-----------------------
| 18:00 — ☀️ Lunch
| — chicken breast 200g raw
| — chicken cooked with 1 tsp of mustard
| — chicken cooked with 5g of olive oil
| — brown rice 15g raw (↓ from 35g)
| — 300g legume sauté with 5g of olive oil (40% carrot, 40% courgette, 20% onion)
| — cumin & salt
-----------------------

18:00 — Black Coffee
18:00
      — (4h) Rest or Billard & Water 1L
21:00
21:00 — Back Home

-----------------------
| 21:00 — 🌙 Dinner
| — 1.5 scoops of protein powder
| — 250g raib (homemade, from Jaouda Sans Lactose)
| — 1 kiwi
| — 5g Almonds
| — 5g Walnuts
| — 5g Cashews
| — 5g Pistachios
| — 5g Pumpkin Seeds
-----------------------

01:00 — Sleep

adjustments.reason: Part of the 2-raib : 1-perly rotation across the 10-day plan. A full 250g serving (not an equal-weight swap) is what actually moves calcium — raib is less calcium-dense than Perly per gram, so 85g would have been a step backward. Calcium rises from 20% to 31%; carbs/fat both nudge up but stay in the good range.

adjustments.from: Brown rice 35g (carbs at 110%, over the new 100% ceiling)
adjustments.reason: New skill rule: carbs over 100% must be trimmed. Chicken's own fat is already low, so this single trim was enough — carbs 110%->99.3%, everything else stays comfortably in its new good range.

verdict.text_html: Re-scored under the new directional rules — 1767 kcal (98%), protein 102%, carbs 99%, fat 96% — all inside the new good bands.

coverage.name: Calories
coverage.note: Re-tuned under the new directional rules (protein floor, carb/fat ceilings)

coverage.name: Protein
coverage.note: See fullTable for the protein-source breakdown

coverage.name: Carbs
coverage.note: Trimmed to sit under the new 100% ceiling

coverage.name: Fat
coverage.note: Trimmed to sit under the new 100% ceiling

coverage.name: Fiber
coverage.note: Oat, legume sauté, apple, and kiwi contribute, but no potato/banana in the rotation

coverage.name: Gas / bloat risk
coverage.note: Bread once, cumin at breakfast and lunch, onion only cooked

coverage.name: Protocol adherence
coverage.note: Bread once ✓, dinner well before 22:00 ✓, cumin at breakfast/lunch ✓

coverage.name: Selenium
coverage.note: Strong across all three rotation versions

good: All four numbers land in the good range — protein 102%, carbs 99%, fat 96%, calories 98%, without needing an extra whey scoop like kefta/thon
Selenium (205%) and Niacin (146%) are chicken's standout strengths
Vitamin A (140%) and Vitamin C (129%) both comfortably covered
Protein clears 165g even before counting the dinner whey bump
bad: Zinc (31%) and Iron (38%) are both weak — chicken carries much less of either than kefta would
Calcium (20%), Vitamin D (10%), Chromium (9%), Vitamin E (23%) — the usual structural gaps
Choline just barely good at 90% — closest to slipping under of the three versions
Fiber still short at ~70% — no potato/banana in the rotation
loggedItems: 6 egg whites + 3 egg yolks (beldi), fried with 5g olive oil
Chicken breast 200g raw, cooked with 1 tsp mustard and 5g olive oil
300g légumes sautés (carrot 120g, courgette 120g, onion 60g) with 5g olive oil
5g Almonds + 5g Walnuts + 5g Cashews + 5g Pistachios + 5g Pumpkin Seeds

fullTable.note: Chicken breast 200g + 1.5 whey scoops + eggs — clears target

Chicken's zinc is much lower than kefta's — the weakest point today
